commands.author: clawsweeper[bot]
commands.review_summary:
This PR adds a repo-local Crabbox skill, Crabbox configuration, a self-hosted hydrate workflow, actionlint runner labels, CODEOWNERS coverage, and package scripts for Crabbox commands.

Reproducibility: yes. for the review findings: source inspection of the proposed workflow shows tag-based action references and no persist-credentials override on checkout. The actual Crabbox lease behavior was not reproduced because the PR body says no live lease was started.
commands.review_followup: The blockers are narrow mechanical workflow hardening edits that automation can attempt before maintainer review of the runner policy.

target.title: chore: add constrained Crabbox setup
target.body:
## Summary

- Adds the exact Crabbox skill copied from `openclaw/openclaw`.
- Adds a constrained Crabbox config and hydrate workflow with repo-specific self-hosted runner labels.
- Adds actionlint label config, CODEOWNERS coverage for the new automation surfaces, and package scripts for the copied skill command surface.

This is the narrowed replacement shape for the earlier broad setup baseline. It intentionally does not add CodeQL, stale automation, licensing changes, Dependabot, or other policy-heavy defaults.

## Verification

- `git diff --check`
- Ruby YAML parse for `.crabbox.yaml`, `.github/actionlint.yaml`, and `.github/workflows/crabbox-hydrate.yml`
- `actionlint -config-file .github/actionlint.yaml .github/workflows/crabbox-hydrate.yml`
- Crabbox skill SHA-256 matched `openclaw/openclaw`: `ed512c0b0385fae7f6c5c14a7e9e6236ab68936506687a99ca976873492bdc43`
- Package script presence check for `check:changed`, `test:changed`, and `crabbox:*`
- Private-path scan for new public files

## Notes

`pnpm run fmt:check` was attempted but pnpm aborted before running checks because the Codex worktree dependency status wanted to purge/reconcile `node_modules` without a TTY. I did not force an install in the worktree. No live Crabbox lease was started for this setup-only patch.
target.author: vincentkoc
